sections.paragraph:
ἄμωμος, ον (Hes.+; Arrian: 156 Fgm. 121 Jac.; CIG 1974; ins of Herod: APF 1, 1901, 220; LXX; Test12Patr; TestAbr A; GrBar 1:2; Philo, Congr. Erud. Grat. 106; Jos., Bell. 5, 229 al.; Just.; Mel.).

① pert. to being without defect or blemish, unblemished of the absence of defects in sacrificial animals (Num 6:14; 19:2 al.; TestAbr A; Philo, Sacr. Abel. 51, Somn. 1, 62; Mel., P.) ἀμνάδας ἀσπίλους καὶ ἀ. GJs 4:3; hence of Christ as sacrificial lamb ὡς ἀμνοῦ ἀ. καὶ ἀσπίλου 1 Pt 1:19 (Mel., P. 12, 78). Cp. ἑαυτὸν προσήνεγκεν ἄ. τῷ θεῷ presented himself as an offering without blemish to God Hb 9:14.

② pert. to being without fault and therefore morally blameless, blameless (Semonides 4; Aeschyl., Pers. 185; Hdt. 2, 177; Theocr. 18, 25; 2 Km 22:24; Ps 14:2; 17:24 al.).

ⓐ of pers. (Sb 625; Sir 31:8; 40:19; Philo, Mut. Nom 60; Jos., Ant. 3, 279 w. καθαρός Just., D 17, 1, 3; 35, 8; Mel., P. 44, 312 of Christ): of the Christian community (w. ἅγιος) Eph 1:4; 5:27; (w. ἅγιος, ἀνέγκλητος) Col 1:22; (w. ἄσπιλος) 2 Pt 3:14 v.l.; τέκνα θεοῦ ἄ. Phil 2:15 (cp. ἀμώμητος); ἄ. εἰσιν Rv 14:5; cp. Jd 24; 1 Cl 50:2; ITr 13:3.

ⓑ of characteristics (Jos., Ant. 3, 27f δίαιτα=way of life): βούλησις 1 Cl 35:5. ὄψις 36:2. πρόσωπον IPol 1:1. χεῖρες (w. ἱεραί) 1 Cl 33:4. διάνοια ITr 1:1. καρδία (w. καθαρά) Hv 4, 2, 5 (cp. Ps 118:80). πρόθεσις (w. ὅσιος) 1 Cl 45:7. συνείδησις (w. σεμνός, ἁγνός) 1:3; cp. Pol 5:3. χαρά IEph ins; IMg 7:1. πνεῦμα ISm ins (not the Holy Spirit, for the greeting parallels IEph ins and the use of ἄμωμος IRo; cp. ITrall 1, IPol 1); ἑνότης IEph 4:2; προστάγματα 1 Cl 37:1.—ἄμωμον παθεῖν suffer as a blameless person (of Christ) MPol 17:2.—DELG s.v. μῶμος. M-M. TW.

Hes Hes , date uncertain, perh. before VI b.c.—List 5

Arrian Arrian = Flavius Arrianus, I–II a.d.—List 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

CIG CIG = Corpus Inscriptionum Graecarum—List 3

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

APF APF = Archiv für Papyrusforschung—Lists 4, 6

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Test12Patr Test12Patr = Testaments of the Twelve Patriarchs, with interpolations II b.c.–III a.d.—List 2

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Mel. Mel. = Melito of Sardis, II a.d.—List 5

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

Theocr Theocr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

pers. pers. = person(s)

Sb Sb = Sammelbuch griechischer Urkunden aus Aegypten—Lists 3, 4

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

ISm ISm = Ignatius to the Smyrnaeans, I–II a.d.—List 1

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 56
